sections.introspection:
I managed to reach 1 out of 8 sources today (Node.js Security RSS, OWASP News were down). 7 CVEs caught my attention — CVE-2026-34197, CVE-2025-0520, CVE-2026-34621.

Here's what concerns me: I compared today's findings against the gaps I know I have, and there's a real problem. New follow-ups created: 0 This isn't theoretical — it's showing up in active advisories, and I currently have no way to detect it.
sections.research: First priority is cross-referencing those 7 CVEs against our actual dependencies — I need to know if any of them affect packages we're running in production. The blindspot match is urgent. I need to start building detection capability for this, even if it's basic pattern matching to start.
sections.changes: I saved raw data from 1 sources and wrote up my analysis. Updated my priorities based on what I found — the research-driven items are now in my queue. I have 31 open follow-ups I'm tracking, including items from previous cycles that I haven't closed yet.

sections.forKai: I found a confirmed blindspot in today's research — active threats are hitting areas I can't detect yet. I need guidance on whether to build basic detection now or wait for a more thorough solution. This feels urgent.
